race_race_name: Prix Esoterique (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares) (Turf)
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Anakova, 3/1 Moon Cloud, 7/1 Never Let Go, 8/1 Breckenbrough,  Gaga Girl, 10/1 Velikaya, 16/1 Chica De Ciudad,  Reine De Medicis, 20/1 Canneza, 33/1 Abiat
runner_quotes: 2025-06-20 - "Kieran's [Shoemark] given Never Let Go a peach. She's a filly we've always believed in and thought this race would be ideal for, and we were not getting in the race at one stage because she didn't win at Wetherby, she didn't win at Haydock, and I thought, 'crikey, we're not going to get in' and I was desperate to run her here, so we ran her in a Listed race, Hollie gave her an absolute peach, she nicked some black type and went up enough to get in, which I was thrilled about. I think she can go on. The family get better with time, she's still very raw still and unfurnished and leggy. She's a homebred of the Keswicks who have supported me for a few years now, and we had a really good year for them last year with Celandine being the highlight - Ed Walker, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "It was good to see her placed in a Listed race at Goodwood the other day, beaten less than a length. She went up 12lb but I still think she's well handicapped for the Sandringham. 10-06-25", "She ran a big race when second in the Snowdrop Stakes at Kempton at 25-1 and should have a good year. We could run her in the Nottinghamshire Oaks on Sunday or the Conqueror Stakes at Goodwood on Guineas weekend and, if we can make her a stakes winner, her big target would be the Duke of Cambridge Stakes at Royal Ascot, where she won the Sandringham last year. 22-04-26"

runner_comment: Placed on Irish point debut and made the frame in all three bumpers; considered

runner_stable_tour: "Bought at the Cheltenham sale in March, she came from the same people who had Bill Joyce and they rated her highly. We'll probably start her off in a bumper in December and go from there. 18-11-25"

runner_comment: Looked promising in bumpers but made disappointing start to hurdling career; new stable
runner_spotlight: Won good-ground Southwell bumper for Ben Pauling in spring 2025 and made the frame in soft-ground Listed event at Cheltenham in November but didn't get far on hurdling debut in December and was remote when third at Lingfield (2m3f, good to soft) later that month; makes stable debut after a break and with a point to prove.
runner_stable_tour: "She's a speedier type than Queenofthejukebox and won nicely on her second start in a bumper at Southwell. We're likely to start her off in the Listed mares' bumper at Cheltenham on November 15. If she's working well enough up to then, we'll be inclined to give her a crack at that and then she'll probably go hurdling. 22-10-25"

race_race_name: KSR Lighting Handicap Hurdle
race_verdict: There is more to come from newly hooded 6yo BEL OMBRE, who won with an indeterminate amount left in the tank on his handicap debut.
race_betting_forecast: 15/8 Oakley, 5/1 Motazzen,  Taritino, 11/2 Bel Ombre, 6/1 Sun Joy, 12/1 Khalk'eau Spigao, 16/1 Hiconic,  Belgarum
runner_breeder: Dr A Bolte U Dr H H Leimbach
runner_prev_trainers: Evan Williams (trn_121059) - 2026-03-13, Anthony Mullins (trn_28521) - 2025-09-22, Dr A Bolte (trn_129051) - 2023-08-15
runner_prev_owners: R E R Williams (own_517776) - 2026-04-29, Mrs P Crampton & Oliver Crampton & DJCC Investments Limited (own_1378028) - 2025-09-22, Crampton Cloudsdale Partners (own_1373064) - 2024-05-09, Mrs P Crampton & O Crampton & D A Cloudsdale & J Crampton (own_1336344) - 2024-04-11, Crampton Partners/D A Cloudsdale (own_1343952) - 2023-10-12
runner_comment: Not always reliable but won easily by over 8l at Hereford last week; carries 7lb penalty
runner_spotlight: 2-7 for current stable since the autumn, the latter win gained easily by over 8l at Hereford (2m3f, good) nine days ago; not always one to rely upon heavily but has obvious claims under 7lb penalty.
runner_stable_tour: "He can be tricky because he needs to be held up, just as he was at Hereford when he won a 2m3½f handicap hurdle in November. He came from last to first that day. He made it look so easy the handicapper raised him 8lb and he's now on a career-high mark. He never threatened at Chepstow the time after that but I thought I had him ready for Warwick on Saturday when the meeting was abandoned. 14-01-26"

race_race_name: KSR Lighting Handicap Hurdle
race_verdict: There is more to come from newly hooded 6yo BEL OMBRE, who won with an indeterminate amount left in the tank on his handicap debut.
race_betting_forecast: 15/8 Oakley, 5/1 Motazzen,  Taritino, 11/2 Bel Ombre, 6/1 Sun Joy, 12/1 Khalk'eau Spigao, 16/1 Hiconic,  Belgarum
runner_comment: No match for winner when second last time but remains very much of interest
runner_spotlight: No match for winner when second at Perth (2m4f, good; raced too freely) last month, after a break, but appeared to have quite a bit in hand when winning on handicap debut at Taunton (2m3f, good to soft) in January and can be dangerous if new hood helps him to relax.
runner_stable_tour: "A very nice horse. He ran a nice race at Huntingdon to finish fourth in a novice hurdle and then went on to Taunton, where he showed an improved performance to win. The more racing he's getting the more confidence he's getting. Certainly, when he ran at Chepstow, before his Huntingdon run, it was a bit too undulating but stepping him up in trip beyond two miles has definitely been the key to him. 04-02-26"

race_race_name: KSR Lighting Handicap Hurdle
race_verdict: There is more to come from newly hooded 6yo BEL OMBRE, who won with an indeterminate amount left in the tank on his handicap debut.
race_betting_forecast: 15/8 Oakley, 5/1 Motazzen,  Taritino, 11/2 Bel Ombre, 6/1 Sun Joy, 12/1 Khalk'eau Spigao, 16/1 Hiconic,  Belgarum
runner_prev_owners: Carol Jackson & Henrietta Knight (own_1395092) - 2024-09-03, Miss H Knight (own_18164) - 2024-08-31, Paul Hudson Racing (own_1291084) - 2024-05-30, Paul Hudson Partnership (own_1267908) - 2022-10-04, Shadwell France (own_581840) - 2022-04-29
runner_comment: Career-best form when winning by 10l here in March; 8lb rise asks a fair bit of him
runner_spotlight: Four-time hurdle winner who rewarded good market support and showed career-best form when drawing clear to score by 10l here (2m5f, good to soft) in March; 8lb rise asks a fair bit of him but the forecast faster ground should not be a problem.
runner_quotes: 2025-01-01 - "Motazzen has his mojo working at the moment, and to be honest he's been well placed, too. The race cut up, but it's still brilliant how he picked up. He was having a good old look around in front - Paul O'Brien, rider, speaking to Sky Sports Racing."
runner_stable_tour: "We acquired this son of Dubawi from the Shadwell dispersal, having won twice in France for Jean-Claude Rouget over 1m2½f and 1m5f. We have had him gelded and he appears to be showing more speed than he did in France. His work has been pleasing and there are a couple of handicaps next week at Chester I have in mind. He came highly recommended from Shadwell, and it made sense for us to buy him. He is a very interesting newcomer to the team. 27-04-22"

runner_comment: Reverts to hurdling on a good mark but has regressed and become inconsistent
runner_spotlight: Came second off basement Flat mark on latest outing in January and is well handicapped for this return to hurdling but she's not as good as she was and has become inconsistent; others appeal more.
runner_quotes: 2020-07-21 - "Hiconic is a hardy filly and stays well, which is key. She's been getting better with each win and we've been schooling her since we claimed her as a 2yo last year. I'm annoyed I didn't enter her at Market Rasen next week but we'll find another opportunity for her and maybe claim off her next time. We'll have a crack at a Listed race down the line as she has nothing to lose - she's been a revelation -  Alex Hales, trainer.", 2020-07-12 - "We've done a lot of schooling with Hiconic and she's always jumped. I was pleased with the way she did it from the front on her own. We'll have to go somewhere with a double penalty - Alex Hales, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "She ran up a string of four straight wins in juvenile hurdles last season as well as finishing second in Listed company. She has started back in her work now and should be ready to run again by the end of February. She needs a trip now, but although a little off a run I like how she is coming on and she can certainly add more wins to her cv. 30-12-21"

Fair play to the course, they must have done a brilliant job with the going because this horse can't go on anything other than slow ground. He's done it well today and he'll go straight chasing now. That trip suited him well, he's a brother to I Am Maximus so he should stay well - Gary Moore, joint-trainer."

runner_stable_tour: "I paid £7,000 for him. He won a race over 1m4f on the Flat before we had him and he's had two starts with us, finishing second in both novice hurdles. race_race_name: V2 Radio Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)
race_verdict: Jennie Candlish sends SAINT POLO on a fairly lengthy journey down to Fontwell and he gets the vote to make a winning chase debut.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Grain Trade, 11/4 Saint Polo, 3/1 Model Approach, 9/1 Gone In Sixty, 10/1 Ballywood, 16/1 Daany, 20/1 Holy Legend,  Millies Mite
runner_breeder: S A R L Ecurie Lafeu
runner_comment: Pipped off this mark two runs back; slightly lesser effort came only one week later
runner_spotlight: Down to this mark when nearly winning for the first time in seven years at Huntingdon in March and a slightly lesser effort when third at Hereford (also 2m, good) came just one week later; not ruled out.
runner_quotes: 2019-03-21 - "Ballywood was meant to run at Cheltenham last week for the Grand Annual Chase but the ground was too soft and we thought we would come here as the prize-money is so good. It's a possibility that he could now go to Aintree or Ayr next month as long as conditions are good - Alan King, trainer.", 2019-01-26 - "We were thrilled with that, Ballywood has run as well as we could have hoped. We will give Cheltenham a thought, he is in the Arkle but he could be a Grand Annual horse - Jason Fildes, Highclere Thoroughbreds northern racing manager.", 2018-12-29 - "Ballywood backed up his win at Taunton and is improving all the time. He jumped well and he's a nippy little horse, two miles round here seemed to suit him - Tom Cannon, jockey.", 2018-12-13 - "He seems a different horse with a fence in front of him. He has been slightly disappointing since he arrived from France but he took a big step forward today. The conditions today helped him and hopefully we have worked out what we need to do with him - Wayne Hutchinson, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He ran some marvellous races last season in a difficult winter given he doesn't want testing ground. He was a good second at Fontwell, where he just needed the run, and may go to Cheltenham on Friday or Aintree on Sunday. I want to try him over two and a half miles at some stage this season. 09-11-20"

runner_spotlight: Won bumpers at Bangor (good) and Stratford (good to firm) last summer; found life tough under a double penalty in a valuable bumper at Newbury back from a break in March but remains a good hurdles prospect, particularly for this time of year.
runner_stable_tour: "He won his first two bumpers really well. He bolted up at Bangor, then did well to defy a penalty at Stratford. I probably brought him back too soon to win it and wasn't 100 per cent right, but he still won. You've got to be quite a good horse to do that. We thought we'd take our time and wait for the big £100,000 bumper at Newbury on Saturday, but he was a little bit disappointing. We couldn't get the job done there, but he's still got an exciting future. 26-03-26"

We put him out in Ruth Jardine's [sister of trainer Iain] field for three years, and it's done the world for him. He's been fantastic ever since, we couldn't complain at all. After he finished third at Haydock we were scratching our heads and wondering where to go, we came here and the next plan will be the Scottish County Hurdle here on February 1. runner_quotes: 2025-05-15 - "It's a track Just Dottie likes. Although Ben [Smith] wasn't riding today, he did tell me of the jockeys available Sean Bowen was the man for it and it'd suit him perfectly. She'll go two or three times in the summer. It was a big step up today to find out just what we've got, but to do that was fantastic - Mike Smith, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.", 2024-05-16 - "I'm very happy that we've got Just Dottie to win it for the second year running. runner_comment: He's run well in defeat over 3m here on his last two starts, most recently yesterday
runner_spotlight: He can make mistakes and may be better over further, but he won two in a row last autumn (2m7f, good/good to soft) and was back on song when runner-up here (3m, good) three weeks ago in reapplied cheekpieces (retained since); good third here (3m, good to soft) yesterday; could be in the mix.
runner_quotes: 2025-10-30 - "It went to plan, and it was lovely to win. Sir Carnegie's jumping keeps improving and he stays very well. If he'd jumped as well at Sedgefield last time, he'd have won. We ran him in a chase at Hexham, but he was jumping a bit novicey, which is why we went back to hurdles. He's had a few falls over hurdles, and you can see he's just getting his confidence up now as he's getting better with every race. He stays all day, and we'll just keep getting him relaxed. He's growing with every run, and hopefully he'll just keep improving - Mickey Bowen, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's bouncing around the place and will be ready in about two weeks. There's a nice 0-100 novice handicap chase at Newcastle and I know it's a long way to travel there from our base in Wales, but we think he can win it. He'll take a lot of beating in it. 26-03-26"

runner_quotes: 2025-06-13 - "We ruined Arabian Cobra last year by running him in the Windsor Castle. It's taken a long time to get his confidence back. But this year working with the juveniles has got him back to himself. That was his second win in a week and if things went right I wouldn't rule out his winning again - Hugo Palmer, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's had a really good year but he never raised a gallop in the Ayr Bronze Cup though, and I don't know why. It's a tough ask for a three-year-old in the older-horse handicaps but we thought it would suit him. He likes give in the ground and it was frustrating. He's in the sales. 08-10-25"

runner_quotes: 2023-09-23 - "Ararat is a talented horse who has underachieved but he's capable on his day - David Simcock, trainer", 2023-06-24 - "Ararat has promised to win in the past so it was nice he could get his head in front. We've tried him on soft ground as he's German-bred, but he likes it quicker-Bruce Raymond, owner's racing manager."

runner_comment: Two fair efforts since returning from a break; form figures here read 3114; holds claims
runner_spotlight: Dual C&D winner last year (including this race), the most recent coming off a 2lb higher mark than today's; fair third at Kempton (7f) on his return and ran a solid race when fourth at Bath 27 days ago; claims back at this track (form figures here read 3114).
runner_stable_tour: "He had lost all his confidence. He had a foot injury, which left him a bit sore for a while, but once he had dropped in grade and we were able to run him in one of the novice rider races with no whips, he won easily and got his confidence back again. He's potentially still well handicapped on his best form but hasn't really come back to his good three-year-old form just yet. 27-08-25"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Apprentice Handicap (Racing Excellence - Whips To Be Carried But Not Used)
race_verdict: The return to this track could suit Safari Dream but MUMAYAZ (nap) won well at Leicester five days ago and looks the one to beat.
race_betting_forecast: 7/4 Mumayaz, 3/1 Safari Dream, 6/1 Strike, 8/1 Forever My Prince, 9/1 Optimatum, 10/1 Ararat, 14/1 Arabian Cobra, 16/1 Distant Rumble, 18/1 Land Of Magic
runner_comment: Two wins from 18 starts; too free at Kempton when last seen 78 days ago; others preferred
runner_spotlight: Two wins from 18 starts; dropping to 5f wouldn't have suited at Lingfield in February but he was too free at Kempton (6f) when last seen 78 days ago; switches back to turf with others preferred.

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_comment: Fair efforts in quite valuable AW h'caps in February/March but disappointed on turf latest
runner_spotlight: Won Newmarket maiden on his debut in 2024 but hasn't kicked on from good juvenile form; fair efforts when fifth in quite valuable AW handicaps in February and March but he proved a disappointing favourite on return to turf when beaten 14l at Beverley (also 2m, good to soft) last month; others hold greater appeal.
runner_quotes: 2024-08-16 - "It was a really pleasing debut. We were a bit unaware of where we were with such a big, fine-looking colt but Anniversary had done enough at home to look ready for his first day out. That was a real taking performance and he had a nice attitude. He's got plenty of size, which is most exciting as there's plenty to look forward to - Rob Hornby, jockey, speaking to Racing TV."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_comment: Lightly raced since 2022 but on a dangerous mark; watch the market on this return
runner_spotlight: Won sole visit here (1m handicap in 2019); in the care of Ian Williams when landing 2m York handicap in August 2022 and ran a mighty race when pipped in the Ebor a fortnight later; pulled up at Chester the following month and seen just three times since, including when down the field in two starts for this yard last year; drops in grade from falling mark; watch the market on return.
runner_quotes: 2022-08-20 - "Alfred Boucher is an absolute star, what a performance. You'd actually have to question how far he'd have won by without the run the other day! But without that run the other day he wouldn't have got in. I'm very proud of him and what he's achieved is absolutely amazing. A little bit more pace in the race would have suited him, but the race was the race. There's lots of targets abroad and we'll see how far we would like to go. He may even come back here next year - Ian Williams, trainer.", 2021-07-23 - "His trainer finally realised Alfred Boucher gets a mile and a half. He's incredibly game. He's got a huge stride, heart and wants to do it. I thought we were being a little bit brave going for this grade of handicap - Henry Candy, trainer."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_comment: Justified support when narrowly beating Cruden over 1m6f latest; unexposed; key player
runner_spotlight: Hurdles winner for this yard; resumed Flat career with close third at Thirsk (1m4f) on handicap debut in this sphere last month and justified strong support when narrowly beating Cruden over this trip at Goodwood (also good) a fortnight ago; unexposed and the best is probably yet to come.
runner_quotes: 2026-02-15 - "I'm delighted with that. It wasn't expected as we were coming here thinking one was going to be very hard to beat. I wanted to get another run into Gallivanted on decent-ish ground. The key was his jumping, he jumped fine at Ludlow but just wasn't fluent. He was much quicker there and got the run of the race. I'm so pleased for his owners. He'll be a progressive horse through the spring, the ground is still not dry enough for him. There's a juvenile handicap at Ascot in April which could be right up his street. There's another one at Kelso on Morebattle [Hurdle] day, and as he likes the northern air we could come back - Harry Derham, trainer, speaking on Racing TV."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_comment: Won (1m6f) off 2lb lower in July 2024; heavy hurdle fall when last seen; market helpful
runner_spotlight: Six-time Flat winner who scored a fifth win on turf when taking Sandown handicap (1m6f, good) off 2lb lower in July 2024; also a multiple winner over hurdles who was on the premises when taking heavy last-flight fall at Plumpton just over one year ago; interesting to see how he goes in the market ahead of this comeback.
runner_quotes: 2024-03-04 - "Mr Freedom is a pleasure to have. People ask me what trip he wants and I don't know. What ground does he want? I don't know either. Cheekpieces on or off? It could be either. It doesn't matter what you do, he always gives his best. It makes him really hard to place, because we don't have parameters to stick to. You're just spoiled for choice, so we try and pick winnable races. Sometimes we get it right, sometimes we don't, but he hasn't done anything wrong this season - Sheena West, trainer.", 2023-07-02 - "Mr Freedom shows virtually nothing at home, which is part of the reason I've just been able to keep going with him. I'd love to know where this horse gets ability from and where the speed comes from. You've actually got to go back four generations in this horse's pedigree before you can find a winner - Sheena West, trainer."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_comment: Sole turf win came over C&D (good to firm); inconsistent of late; threat if on a going day
runner_spotlight: Three-time winner whose sole success on turf came over C&D (good to firm) just under one year ago; also scored off this mark on AW (1m6f) in September but has been very in and out on last four starts, including when tried in a hood (removed) and beaten 32l in valuable AW event last time; drops back down in grade from lower turf mark and can make presence felt if on a going day.
runner_stable_tour: "He's probably the best three-year-old we've got in the yard. He's really good and works very well and I'll be disappointed if he doesn't get rated in the 90s. He ran once last year when he finished third at Newcastle. We've had issues with the stalls with him as he's a big horse and he gave away six lengths at the start at Newcastle but was only beaten three lengths. The winner won again, so the form is all right. He's improved loads since last year. He's ready to go. 03-04-24"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_comment: Big run for yard when giving Gallivanted a fright (1m6f, good; 25-1) latest; contender
runner_spotlight: Won maiden Flat and hurdle race for Jessie Harrington in 2024; big run for this yard when keeping on well from the rear to give Gallivanted a fright at Goodwood (1m6f, good; 25-1) a fortnight ago; had to come down the outer that day and it's no guarantee his old adversary will confirm those placings.
runner_quotes: 2024-05-16 - "Cruden is a big horse who's really starting to fill into himself and is learning on the job. When Shane got him out into the open he didn't really know what to do but he really ground it out to the line - Kate Harrington, assistant trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "This colt by Time Test hasn't started out yet, but he is one we like and we're looking forward to getting him out onto the track. 24-04-24"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_owner: Hot To Trot 1 & Mrs R Pease
runner_comment: Solid efforts last year and won (1m6f, soft; acts on good) in June; respected on return
runner_spotlight: Won sole start at two (heavy) and a series of solid efforts from 1m3f to 1m6f last year included win at Ffos Las (1m6f, soft; acts on good) in June; rain would likely increase her chances but still respected on this reappearance.
runner_quotes: 2024-10-08 - "We had to talk the co-owner into running Personal Best. Dear Rosa Pease, she's been with me for 26 years and she's the breeder of Personal Best. She probably told me about six times not to run. Thank God the filly has done her well. I just thought they'd need the run today. On this [heavy] ground, you don't know quite what's going to happen. They've not had anything too demanding asked of them at home so I didn't have huge expectations -  Jonathan Portman, trainer, speaking on Racing TV."
runner_stable_tour: "He's a lovely staying type who ideally would want a bit of give in the ground. She disappointed a bit at Sandown, but I think she's a bit better than that and hopefully she'll be out when there's a bit more ease in the ground. She's a nice horse to follow. 20-08-25"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai "City Bowl" Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
race_verdict: Gallivanted and CRUDEN fought out a good duel last time and the latter is taken to gain his revenge in this historic handicap.
race_betting_forecast: 5/2 Gallivanted, 4/1 Cruden, 5/1 Personal Best, 11/2 Anniversary, 13/2 Mr Freedom, 7/1 Just An Hour, 12/1 Tryfan, 25/1 Alfred Boucher, 50/1 Individualiste
runner_comment: Last won at 1m2f but runner-up over C&D last term and again over 1m6f 18 days ago; player
runner_spotlight: Four-time winner who last scored over 1m2f at Windsor (good to firm) off just 1lb lower in July; runner-up over C&D in October, however, and confirmed this trip holds no fears when second again at Nottingham (also good) 18 days ago; player.
runner_stable_tour: "He was one of the few failures I've had, until this year. When we've been lucky enough to get hold of nice horses from other yards we've usually done very well with them. He was a three-year-old at Joseph O'Brien's and won his maiden and, when he came to me, he'd been gelded and was quite light. I was hoping to sweeten him up and have a good campaign last year, but it's taken me a whole season to get him back to form, which he's done with a vengeance, winning three times. He's looking good now and ran a cracking race at Windsor last time in a very competitive Racing League contest. There should be more to come from him but when you win three in one year, it's hard to win the fourth. 27-08-25"

runner_quotes: 2024-12-15 - "If anything Individualiste probably travelled a little too easily. I thought he got left there [in front] a little bit soon and he probably idled a little bit but when Charlie [O'Dwyer, rider of the runner-up] came to me I always felt like I was holding him. His confidence seems to be getting up and hopefully he can keep improving - Keith Donoghue, jockey, speaking on Racing TV."
runner_stable_tour: "He'd been off the track for over 18 months before reappearing at Newton Abbot last month and understandably got a bit tired after travelling well for a long way. He's sure to come on for that and is up to winning handicap hurdles round the smaller tracks. 05-11-23"

runner_spotlight: Third in a bumper at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month when trained by Michael Keady and he didn't run badly when fifth on his Flat/stable debut at Southwell (1m4f, AW) 19 days ago; drops in trip and may be one for handicaps.

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld, Dubai Novice Stakes (Colts & Geldings)
race_verdict: 200,000gns yearling Ancestor is interesting on his debut but Archie Watson's WAREETH sets a good standard and is the choice.
race_betting_forecast: 8/13 Wareeth, 3/1 Ancestor, 8/1 Pacific Storm, 12/1 Who's Lope, 20/1 Ramli, 25/1 Brother Jack
runner_comment: Looked a nice prospect on his debut but found Listed company too much latest; up to 1m2f
runner_spotlight: Sea The Stars colt who cost 510,000gns as a yearling; looked a nice prospect when scoring by 14l on his debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in March but found the step up to Listed company too much in the Feilden Stakes 29 days ago, albeit he was keeping on again at the line which bodes well for this slightly longer trip; carries a penalty but sets the standard returned to a novice.
runner_quotes: 2026-03-21 - "We haven't had many by Sea The Stars, but for Wareeth to do it in that fashion on debut I was pretty impressed. We've always liked him a lot. He's a typical Sea The Stars - quite workmanlike at home - and we thought he'd run well, but to do it like that was a big surprise. He was very green going to post and it was important to get him out today before we step him on to turf so he can learn plenty. I like the way he knuckled down and hit the line hard as he was lonely up there. He's always been quite raw and immature, so to have been so professional on debut was nice. I think that's his minimum trip. I'm not sure what Archie has planned, but we needed to get over this hurdle today to see what we're going to do going forward - Hollie Doyle, jockey, speaking on Sky Sports Racing."

race_race_name: Lindum York Handicap
race_verdict: The drop back to 5f could be ideal for topweight HAMMER THE HAMMER and he is taken to make a winning reappearance.
race_betting_forecast: 9/2 Corolla Point, 6/1 Air Force One, 8/1 Hammer The Hammer, 9/1 Redorange, 11/1 Arklow Lad, 12/1 Ziggy's Triton,  Jakajaro, 14/1 Luna A Inbhir Nis,  Sturlasson, 16/1 Against The Wind,  Heavenly Heather,  Brazen Bolt, 20/1 Toca Madera,  Eternal Sunshine,  I'm Next,  Copper Knight, 25/1 Stormy Impact, 33/1 Squealer,  Bergerac,  Lethal Nymph,  Sports Coach, 40/1 Blinky
runner_comment: Showed good speed over 6f here last October and the return to 5f could be a positive move
runner_spotlight: Second of 24 in the 5f 3yo handicap at Royal Ascot (5f, good to firm) last June and he showed good speed before fading into sixth of 19 over 6f here (good) last October, when last seen; wind op since; the drop back in trip could be a positive move on his reappearance; can make a bold bid.
runner_quotes: 2025-06-20 - "Delighted with Hammer The Hammer. The ground was always a concern coming into today. Not taking anything away from the winner, but I think he would be better just getting a toe in a little bit - Tom Eaves, jockey.", 2025-05-07 - "Hammer The Hammer is one we've held in such high regard. Stall ten, he's got great gate speed so we had to make the decision to jump and go or take back and he jumped the gates well. He's a very talented horse and he'll keep improving. I'd probably look to go for the handicap [Wokingham] at Royal Ascot with him - Kevin Ryan, trainer."

race_race_name: Lindum York Handicap
race_verdict: The drop back to 5f could be ideal for topweight HAMMER THE HAMMER and he is taken to make a winning reappearance.

runner_comment: Comfortable win at Newmarket on reappearance; remains well treated on historic exploits
runner_spotlight: Snapped a lengthy losing sequence when making virtually all to win at Newmarket (5f, good) last month on his reappearance; he needs to back up that performance but did it comfortably last time and, following a 5lb rise, remains well treated on his historic exploits; a possible.
runner_quotes: 2026-04-14 - "It wasn't the plan to make the running with Jakajaro but Jamie walked the track beforehand and the far side was the place to be. It's nice that he's retained all his old abilities - Robert Cowell, trainer.", 2023-07-22 - "Jakajaro was a horse I thought would win a maiden. This is a good horse. He was still green today and hopefully he will go on. He's in the big sales race here in September and that will be in his target - David Marnane, trainer"

race_race_name: Lindum York Handicap
race_verdict: The drop back to 5f could be ideal for topweight HAMMER THE HAMMER and he is taken to make a winning reappearance.
race_betting_forecast: 9/2 Corolla Point, 6/1 Air Force One, 8/1 Hammer The Hammer, 9/1 Redorange, 11/1 Arklow Lad, 12/1 Ziggy's Triton,  Jakajaro, 14/1 Luna A Inbhir Nis,  Sturlasson, 16/1 Against The Wind,  Heavenly Heather,  Brazen Bolt, 20/1 Toca Madera,  Eternal Sunshine,  I'm Next,  Copper Knight, 25/1 Stormy Impact, 33/1 Squealer,  Bergerac,  Lethal Nymph,  Sports Coach, 40/1 Blinky
runner_comment: Reappears on a competitive mark and Ryan Moore has excellent 33% strike-rate for Clive Cox
runner_spotlight: Last season's 3yo campaign ended with two below-par runs but he may have been ready for a break; reappears on a competitive mark based on last summer's form in big-field 5f handicaps and Ryan Moore, who has an excellent 33% strike-rate for Clive Cox, is booked; could be involved.
runner_quotes: 2025-05-07 - "They've gone really hard and the draw was very helpful. Redorange has got a great mind for a sprinter and most importantly so much speed. We're really excited about him - he's very talented. He's improved from two to three and gelding him might have helped. He could be a horse we're thinking about for the [Palace Of] Holyroodhouse at Royal Ascot - Clive Cox, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He progressed with every run last year and won at Yarmouth on good to firm ground on his final run. He's come to himself nicely this spring - his work has been very good. We'll be seeing him very soon over five or six furlongs. 17-04-25"

race_race_name: Lindum York Handicap
race_verdict: The drop back to 5f could be ideal for topweight HAMMER THE HAMMER and he is taken to make a winning reappearance.
race_betting_forecast: 9/2 Corolla Point, 6/1 Air Force One, 8/1 Hammer The Hammer, 9/1 Redorange, 11/1 Arklow Lad, 12/1 Ziggy's Triton,  Jakajaro, 14/1 Luna A Inbhir Nis,  Sturlasson, 16/1 Against The Wind,  Heavenly Heather,  Brazen Bolt, 20/1 Toca Madera,  Eternal Sunshine,  I'm Next,  Copper Knight, 25/1 Stormy Impact, 33/1 Squealer,  Bergerac,  Lethal Nymph,  Sports Coach, 40/1 Blinky
runner_comment: Has run well in big field over C&D but it's possible he's superior on AW; has had wind op
runner_spotlight: Third of 22 in 3yo handicap over C&D (good to firm) at this meeting 12 months ago and progressive on AW since; soft ground may not have suited at Musselburgh last time and he's had wind surgery since; however, a career best is needed today and he has to prove he's just as good on turf as AW.
runner_quotes: 2024-06-29 - "We thought the world of Against The Wind into the spring. I was very confident he'd go and win at York first time as he was so professional at home but he got beat. First run you can always forgive with a little bit of greenness but he then got beaten again on his second start. I felt just dropping back to five [furlongs] and taking a lead would suit and he showed his quality today - Kevin Ryan, trainer."

race_race_name: Lindum York Handicap
race_verdict: The drop back to 5f could be ideal for topweight HAMMER THE HAMMER and he is taken to make a winning reappearance.
race_betting_forecast: 9/2 Corolla Point, 6/1 Air Force One, 8/1 Hammer The Hammer, 9/1 Redorange, 11/1 Arklow Lad, 12/1 Ziggy's Triton,  Jakajaro, 14/1 Luna A Inbhir Nis,  Sturlasson, 16/1 Against The Wind,  Heavenly Heather,  Brazen Bolt, 20/1 Toca Madera,  Eternal Sunshine,  I'm Next,  Copper Knight, 25/1 Stormy Impact, 33/1 Squealer,  Bergerac,  Lethal Nymph,  Sports Coach, 40/1 Blinky
runner_comment: Front-runner who was a highly progressive 3yo last season, winning here; could go well
runner_spotlight: Front-runner who was a highly progressive 3yo last season, making it six wins for the campaign when scoring here (5.4f, good) last July; very close third of 19 over this C&D (good to firm) next time; suited by this course and could give a good account on her reappearance.
runner_quotes: 2025-07-05 - "Luna A Inbhir Nis is just fantastic. Her owners were patient with her at the backend and they've reaped the rewards now. Her knees were a little bit weak, so we put her away. She has a few options and I'd ideally like to keep her to three-year-old races - Katie Scott, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "She came to us just over a year ago unbroken and was our first horse for Summerstorm Bloodstock. She was a bit green - she's a big filly and was just learning at the backend of last year - her knees were a bit immature and she had a bit of time off, but she has just blossomed, winning five of her seven races this year, going from strength to strength. The sky is the limit at the minute - the dream is alive so we'll see how we go. At the moment we've got the Sunday Series race at Haydock pencilled in at the start of August. That would be her first run against older horses so it would be quite a step up for her, but that's what we've got half an eye on. If she kept going the way she's going we'd have to look for some black type for her. 17-07-25"

race_race_name: Lindum York Handicap

runner_comment: Two wins last season, including the 3yo Dash at Epsom; the strong pace will help today
runner_spotlight: Won the big-field 3yo Dash at Epsom (5f, good) last June and landed another quality handicap at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in July; she lacks a recent run and there is a suspicion that 6f is her optimum trip, but she'll get a strong pace to aim at today and is not ruled out off a competitive mark.
runner_quotes: 2025-06-07 - "At halfway I think we were last, or second-last; they must have run very quick because Stormy Impact is not slow, and she was struggling to lay up. I did think she could run well here. I was struggling to find a race for her -I had to run her over six at Newmarket and I think five is her trip. She's all speed. I think she will go for the Palace of Holyrood [on the Friday of Royal Ascot over five furlongs] - Richard Fahey, trainer."

race_race_name: Lindum York Handicap
race_verdict: The drop back to 5f could be ideal for topweight HAMMER THE HAMMER and he is taken to make a winning reappearance.

runner_comment: Has been doing well this year over 5f, at which she is unexposed; each-way contender
runner_spotlight: 5yo who raced over further until this year, and her three career-best RPRs have come on her three attempts at 5f; kept on well for close fourth of 14 at Musselburgh (5f, soft) last month on a rare outing on turf, and she's an each-way contender if handling the forecast better ground.
runner_quotes: 2025-04-18 - "We always knew Heavenly Heather was a really good horse. It was just getting her fit enough. She's such a big heavy mare she takes that much work getting fit. She's needed the race before and she's been green and backward. She's had loads of graft in the last month. I thought she would be sixth, if you took the first two out it's a basic Class 4 handicap. I knew there was improvement in her - Tracy Waggott, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "The time before she won at Newcastle at 200-1, she was badly drawn at Redcar and finished ninth. That was her first run since January and she ran well considering the draw and that she was running against some old, experienced handicappers. She's a big, heavy-topped filly and they take some learning as you daren't push them at home. She was fourth at Carlisle on Monday on her first run since the Newcastle win and it's possible she could go back there for the Carlisle Bell. 21-05-25"

runner_spotlight: Trainer won this last year with subsequent Group 1 winner American Affair; progressive last season and she's 1lb lower than when a close third of 18 over C&D (good) last October; however, well beaten on both starts this season (irregular heartbeat last time) and a major turnaround is needed.
runner_quotes: 2025-09-19 - "Eternal Sunshine just took a false step coming out of the stalls. She probably did a bit too much through the first furlong because I only had one iron, other than that she's run a blinder - Lauren Young, rider.", 2025-09-13 - "Eternal Sunshine has been running really well, she was full of confidence and she was carrying no weight at all. She picked up and quickened away nicely - Lauren Young, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "She's had a really good season, winning five times. It was frustrating to get beat last time when she got hampered in the Ayr Bronze Cup. She's a homebred and we purposely let her mature. I still think there's improvement in her but we've decided that was enough for the season. Hopefully she'll come back off a nice mark next year and we'll look to follow in the footsteps of American Affair. 02-10-24"

runner_quotes: 2025-07-29 - "It means the absolute world, especially on Brazen Bolt, he's a really nice horse. I won on him last time at York, came here today and I thought we had a really nice chance. When the rain started coming I got a little bit worried but he showed what he can do and he's won lovely - Warren Fentiman, jockey.", 2023-09-09 - "It was fairly easy, I just jumped out of the stalls and sat with him. Brazen Bolt was happy there and we didn't complicate it. He's an older horse and knows what he's doing - Gianluca Sanna, jockey, speaking to Racing TV."
runner_stable_tour: "His owners would like to go for the Dash at Epsom on Derby day, but in the last couple of years he might not have got in off his current handicap mark, so maybe he'll have to win or run well before then to make the cut - that'll be the plan of action. I don't see why we don't go to Goodwood again with him in the summer. He's a straightforward sprinter. He's just the sort of horse we like here, being a good horse who just needed a change of scenery, a change of regime and a bit of tender loving care. 15-04-26"

runner_comment: Prolific York winner; scored at Beverley latest; however, this 12yo may find this too hot
runner_spotlight: Hugely admirable 12yo whose seven York wins include this race in 2019 and 2021, and he built upon last month's reappearance second at Ripon (5f, good) to win at Beverley (5f, good to soft) ten days ago; however, there's a suspicion he may find a big-field Class 2 such as this too hot nowadays.
runner_quotes: 2025-06-14 - "Copper Knight is a super old boy and a proper racehorse. It's fantastic to win on him after all this time. I've had a few placings on him and he's been around the Easterbys for a long time, but it's a delight to ride him and ride a winner on him - Duran Fentiman, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.", 2025-06-09 - "It's a fantastic result all round. Copper Knight is a real yard superstar and you couldn't predict what he's going to do - he's a legend - William Easterby, trainer's assistant.", 2023-07-14 - "Copper Knight is a wonderful little horse and tries like hell. He just loves it here and a bit of juice in the ground - Tim Easterby, trainer.", 2022-06-11 - "It's unbelievable and I'm not sure how Copper Knight keeps showing up and putting in these amazing performances. He started the season quite high in the handicap and needed to come down a few pounds, but he's still full of it and we'll keep going back to York as he absolutely loves it there - Tom Denham, part-owner.", 2021-08-18 - "Copper Knight is a super horse and wonderful to train. Dave (Allan) said he'd not felt as good as that in a long time. He loves good ground and coasting along. He trains himself really, he just lobs away at the back and we don't do anything fancy with him. He gets himself ready and is an absolute star. We may look at the Portland at Doncaster - Tim Easterby, trainer.", 2021-05-13 - "Copper Knight is a star. He ran great first time out last year then he went up 7lb, that snookered him and he went off the boil so he got himself re-handicapped.   He ran well at Chester last week and they dropped him 1lb for being third but he likes it here. He was just cocking his lugs a bit but he was always going to hold on.   We might run him in the Dash at Epsom - Tim Easterby, trainer.", 2019-07-13 - "Copper Knight is a lovely horse, he's been an absolute star for us and he's better than ever. Ideally he'd wants a bit more juice in the ground but he copes with anything. He's in the King George Stakes at Goodwood and he'll probably go there and he could come back here for the Nunthorpe - Tim Easterby, trainer.", 2019-05-16 - "Copper Knight is a wonderful horse, he's one of those that tries, he wants to win races. I'd rather have been drawn low because I think the high stalls had a disadvantage but he ran great. He's a super little horse and he'll go to Epsom for the Dash. Eeh Bah Gum will go too, he ran well but he wasn't drawn where the pace was - Tim Easterby, trainer.", 2018-11-07 - "I rode Copper Knight last time at Doncaster and he ran a really good race. Today the race worked out perfectly for him. He wants a strong pace and when the gaps opened he took them - Paul Hanagan, rider.", 2018-10-12 - "Copper Knight was niggled a bit early on so they must have been going fast. He likes a bit of ease in the ground - Tim Easterby, trainer.", 2018-06-29 - "Copper Knight is pretty uncomplicated. He just jumps fast. He seemed to be really enjoying himself in front, he felt like he was at half pace - I was ahead of some quick horses but he felt like he was doing it easily. I fancied him quite a lot for the Scottish Sprint last time but I think he probably just still needed the run to put the fitness on him - David Allan, jockey.", 2017-05-27 - "Copper Knight isn't very big but he's very quick. He could be a Listed performer - Keith Stone, trainer's representative.", 2017-05-12 - "Copper Knight ran a good race at Ripon last month. He's won round Chester before and seems to be getting faster - Anthony Denham, part-owner.", 2016-05-04 - "We were hopeful at Windsor, but Copper Knight ran into one of Richard Hannon's. We entered him just to see what would turn up against him and luckily the ground dried a bit and the race could have been stronger, though it wasn't weak. Royal Ascot would be the dream, but we'll have to see - George Boughey, assistant trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He looks great and you'd never know he was ten years old. He'll go back to York for the Dante meeting. He's won seven times there - some hate the track but he loves it. 23-04-24"

runner_spotlight: Won off 7lb higher over C&D (good) last June and he's one to watch in the betting; an underwhelming autumn was followed by a fairly encouraging reappearance run at Beverley last month, but he was down the field at Thirsk last time and is back to having something to prove.
runner_quotes: 2025-06-28 - "With the second winning at Epsom, it swapped the weights around a bit and it's brilliant to win at our local track. Squealer has only got one run of 100 yards and I'm not sure where we'll go next. We'll see what the handicapper does - Nigel Tinkler, trainer.", 2023-08-19 - "I didn't quite think Squealer would pick up like he did, he hit a little bit of a flat spot then he picked up very well in the end. He's got an electric turn of foot when you can get him to switch off in the first half of the race. He wants top-of-the-ground. He's been a bit unlucky a few times but now he's got his head in front I think he'll grow in confidence. He's a nice sprinter going forward - Rowan Scott, jockey.", 2022-09-04 - "Squealer is a lovely colt who has done little wrong in his career so far. It was a good place to win today. He'll go to the Cornwallis Stakes next as there aren't many other races left this season for horses like him - Nigel Tinkler, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "His win at York in June meant a lot to us as it's our local course and the owners have an apartment in the old Terry's Chocolate factory, which overlooks the track. He beat Jm Jungle that day and that form looks even better now. The handicapper has got his measure a bit and he's been unlucky with his draws in recent races. He'll go back to York for the final meeting there but he needs quick ground. He won't go to Bahrain this time as he didn't travel well there last year. 01-10-25"

race_race_name: Oakmere Homes Hambleton Handicap
race_verdict: Topweight POINT LYNAS has a very solid record at York, including an all-the-way win in this in 2024, and he can capitalise on stall 1.
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Sea Force, 11/2 Shout, 13/2 Cerulean Bay, 9/1 Mirsky, 11/1 Walsingham,  Maybe Not, 12/1 Point Of Contact, 14/1 Point Lynas,  First Principle,  Diego Ventura, 16/1 Vafortino, 25/1 Godwinson,  Galeron,  Theoryofeverything,  Old Cock, 33/1 Eldrickjones,  Thunder Roar, 40/1 Duke's Command, 66/1 Croupier
runner_owner: Julie And David R Martin And Dan Hall
runner_prev_owners: Julie Martin & David R Martin & Partner (own_848336) - 2022-05-26
runner_comment: Strong record at York, including in this race; capable of very bold bid from inside stall
runner_spotlight: Below best on final two starts last season and same story on sole run this term, in Qatar in February; also has a hefty mark to defy but this front-runner was a fine second under an enterprising ride in a Group 2 at Ascot last July and has a strong record here, finishing second in this race in 2023 and winning it in 2024; capable of very bold bid from the inside stall.
runner_quotes: 2024-07-28 - "I didn't think Point Lynas would do it quite as well as that but he had a good draw and he loves this ground. Everyone let him do his own thing but he just gets into a rhythm and keeps going. He's in the Celebration Mile [at Goodwood] and there's also the Strensall at York - and he loves that place. He'll get an entry and, wherever the ground is quickest, he'll go - Ed Bethell, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.", 2024-05-16 - "I was thinking we were going a bit fast but Point Lynas just has one way of going, you just let him do it. Don't fight him. Callum gets on with him great. He deserved to win a nice one like this. I'd say that was a career-best performance. We'll probably look at something a little better than a handicap now, we'll probably have to I imagine - Ed Bethell, trainer.", 2021-10-09 - "Point Lynas has been unlucky not to win before now, he has tended to over-race, but he's settled nicely in front today. He's in the horses-in-training sale and would make a fun horse for someone - Barry Hills, spokesman for winning trainer Charlie."
runner_stable_tour: "He joined us last year and has gone from strength to strength. By the end of his first season with us he had gone up to a mark of 90, and this term he won at Newcastle first time out before putting up a career-best effort last time when second to Croupier in the Hambleton at York's May meeting off 93. The handicapper has nudged him up 2lb for that, but he's absolutely thriving and all roads lead to Royal Ascot for him. He's a funny horse at home as he does just as he wants, but we all love him. He's not over-big but has a great action and loves top of the ground, which hopefully it will be at Ascot. Another race I'd love to run him in is the Golden Mile at Glorious Goodwood as he loves those big-field handicaps with a strong pace. 01-06-23"

runner_quotes: 2022-09-24 - "We were worried about the draw, worried about the ground but he's just done it. Me and my wife are elated. I thought Galleron was unlucky at Doncaster. I was hoping for a place and he came fourth, just was half a length behind the third - Gary Robinson, owner."
runner_stable_tour: "The Hampton Court Stakes is where we could try him, but I need to speak to the owners before confirming it. I'd love to see him over a mile and a quarter ? we've got to bite the bullet and try it one day, so why not here? He finished fourth in the 2,000 Guineas and was fifth in the Irish Guineas, although he finished closer to the winner than at Newmarket. Both times he hit the line really well and should get the extra distance on the way he races. 13-06-23", "He won twice last year including the Goffs Million, where he earned his owners ¤611,000. He has run well without success this season, notably when a good fourth to Chaldean in the 2,000 Guineas. His subsequent effort at the Curragh in the Irish Guineas could have seen him finish much closer if things had gone his way. I know he's better than that fifth behind Paddington and his subsequent ninth in the St James's Palace Stakes on Tuesday. 21-06-23"

He travelled well into the race after overcoming a bit of greenness early on. He's got a really good mind on him, let's hope he can build on it - Robert Havlin, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He looked really good one day at Ascot last year when he was held up on soft ground. He definitely needs it slow and that will be a big factor for him. His work is probably better than it was last year. 14-04-25"

runner_prev_owners: Mrs David Aykroyd (own_632180) - 2026-04-13, Mr and Mrs David Aykroyd (own_591340) - 2025-05-09
runner_comment: Won this off 1lb lower last year but arrives with something to prove this time around
runner_spotlight: Won this off 1lb lower last year (good to firm); however, soundly beaten over C&D (good to firm) last August on final run for Ed Bethell and down the field on last month's stable/seasonal debut at Newbury (1m, good).
runner_quotes: 2025-05-15 - "The trainer f***ed up by running Old Cock in the Lincoln. He got a rush of blood early in the year and ran him too soon. He wasn't quite there and I looked at him on the Sunday morning after the race and said I'm a f***ing idiot. This has been the aim since and I'm delighted we were able to come here and run a good race. He's very talented on his day and he looked a million going round the parade ring today, I was never confident because it's York but I had a strong hope. Who knows where the ceiling will be with him? He's a big horse with plenty of scope so we'll see. We'll aim towards the Hunt Cup but I'll speak to the owners before making a final decision - Ed Bethell, trainer.", 2024-08-10 - "Old Cock is a lovely horse and always has been. The first two home were the right two and he's a big, raw horse who could be even better next year. There's a 3yo 1m handicap at York in two weeks I had in the back of my mind, but he doesn't really take his racing that well. He's a big horse and he's not great when you rush him, so we'll see how he is - Ed Bethell, trainer."
runner_stable_tour: "He's a legend, a really great horse for his owners and still pretty progressive. We decided the Hunt Cup probably wasn't his race, or Ascot his course. He's better on flat tracks and the Hunt Cup is a bit of a lottery. So he'll go back to York on July 12 for a 0-100 handicap over a mile then he'll be trained for the Sky Bet Mile at the Ebor meeting. That's his sort of track so we'll play to his strengths. I think he needs proper decent ground. 02-07-25"

race_race_name: Oakmere Homes Hambleton Handicap
race_verdict: Topweight POINT LYNAS has a very solid record at York, including an all-the-way win in this in 2024, and he can capitalise on stall 1.

runner_comment: Did it cosily at Pontefract last time and 4lb rise may not stop this thriving 6yo
runner_spotlight: Ran just four times last year, winning on three occasions (1m, good/soft), and back on the scoresheet at Pontefract (1m, good) a fortnight ago on his second start of the season; did it cosily last time and a 4lb rise may not stop this thriving 6yo.
runner_quotes: 2026-04-29 - "The last couple of springs haven't gone smoothly but Walsingham had a clean run through this time and I hope he can kick on. When we bought him in 2023 we thought he could be a proper handicapper - Martin Dixon, of The Horse Watchers.", 2025-09-05 - "Walsingham jumped really well and took a strong hold, Danny Tudhope said he can race lazily but he didn't today. He travelled into it really well, but I could have done with something taking me into the race a bit longer because when he hit the front he didn't do too much. He's done it nicely. I know it's hard to get home here at Ascot on soft ground, so I was keeping an eye on the leaders but he was always giving me such a good feel that I knew I'd get them back - Brodie Hampson, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "We paid 50,000gns for him around this time last year but he hasn't run for us yet. He was working very well in the spring and then had a little setback just as he was ready to run. He had some strong maiden form in Ireland when he was trained by Dermot Weld and he's definitely well handicapped. He runs at Redcar on Wednesday and it might take him a run or two to get back after his long time off. He'd be one to follow as I'm pretty sure there's mileage in his mark. 26-09-24"

race_race_name: Oakmere Homes Hambleton Handicap
race_verdict: Topweight POINT LYNAS has a very solid record at York, including an all-the-way win in this in 2024, and he can capitalise on stall 1.

runner_comment: Lightly raced 4yo who has been gelded and may have more to offer for his good yard
runner_spotlight: Respectable efforts last July-September after moving into handicaps, the best run coming over C&D (good to firm) in August when close fifth of 17; gelded since last seen and it's possible this lightly raced 4yo will have more to offer for his good yard this term.

race_race_name: Oakmere Homes Hambleton Handicap
race_verdict: Topweight POINT LYNAS has a very solid record at York, including an all-the-way win in this in 2024, and he can capitalise on stall 1.
