runner_spotlight: Three wins last June-September (7f-1m) along with 1l second of 18 in a fillies' handicap here (7f, good to firm), and she's better than she showed at Newmarket (1m, good) on her final outing last season; this lightly raced 4yo is in excellent hands to continue to progress and she's one to consider.

race_race_name: Oakmere Homes Hambleton Handicap
race_verdict: Topweight POINT LYNAS has a very solid record at York, including an all-the-way win in this in 2024, and he can capitalise on stall 1.
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Sea Force, 11/2 Shout, 13/2 Cerulean Bay, 9/1 Mirsky, 11/1 Walsingham,  Maybe Not, 12/1 Point Of Contact, 14/1 Point Lynas,  First Principle,  Diego Ventura, 16/1 Vafortino, 25/1 Godwinson,  Galeron,  Theoryofeverything,  Old Cock, 33/1 Eldrickjones,  Thunder Roar, 40/1 Duke's Command, 66/1 Croupier
runner_prev_trainers: Roger Fell & Sean Murray (trn_364671) - 2025-05-05, Roger Fell (trn_291465) - 2023-06-12
runner_prev_owners: Nick Bradley Racing 42,Salthouse&partner (own_1218832) - 2023-04-29, Nick Bradley Racing 42 & Partners (own_1218772) - 2021-05-29, Nick Bradley Racing 42 & Partner (own_1217640) - 2021-05-28
runner_comment: Ran okay when third of six at Southwell last month but looks vulnerable back up in grade
runner_spotlight: Well handicapped on last year's best form (including on turf) and ran okay when third of six at Southwell (1m, AW) last month; however, he's been hard to win with on grass and looks vulnerable back up in grade.
runner_quotes: 2021-06-15 - "Eldrickjones is a horse that we liked a lot at home, so this performance was no surprise. I'm delighted with him, he's still a baby, is raw and green. There's still lots of improvement in him - Daniel Tudhope, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "He could end up somewhere similar to La Trinidad as a miler and there could be a nice race for him at Newcastle on June 27 - it's a 0-105 over a mile. We think he's turned into a bit of a specialist at that track. Before that, though, he has a big day at Royal Ascot on Wednesday as he is going in the Royal Hunt Cup. Last summer the owner suggested giving him a wind operation and we haven't really looked back since. He had a good winter and he won at Newcastle last month, but York last time probably came a little bit too quick for him. I definitely think there's scope for him to improve. He showed lots of potential as a two-year-old, when he was second in the Coventry, and we're hoping he can land a big handicap this year. 21-06-24"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: It's possible last year's winner SEE THE FIRE will have a fitness edge over reappearing pair Fallen Angel and Dancing Rain.
race_betting_forecast: 6/5 See The Fire, 11/4 Fallen Angel, 3/1 Diamond Rain, 7/1 Red Letter
runner_comment: Top mare but the trip, an absence and 3lb penalty are possible reasons to oppose her
runner_spotlight: Won three 1m Group 1s against her own sex last year, namely the Prix Rothschild, Matron and Sun Chariot (all good/good to yielding); tackles 1m2f for just the second time on her seasonal return (wasn't strong at the finish when fourth in the 2024 Prix de l'Opera on previous attempt but it was only just short of her best RPR at the time); has to carry a 3lb penalty and wasn't at her best on her reappearance in 2024 or 2025; top mare but there are reasons to oppose her this time.
runner_quotes: 2025-10-04 - "The ground was so quick at [Royal] Ascot and you could see Fallen Angel's head came up coming round the bend. As long as there is no jar in the ground, she is fine but when there is jar in the ground, she just doesn't let herself down. I was more than hopeful she would come back but I would be lying if I said I thought she would win three Group Ones on the bounce. She got very idle at home and was only going through the motions. We needed to sharpen her up and she worked very well in them [cheekpieces] the first time we used them, so it was a no-brainer not to put them on her at Ascot and then after that we knew it was the ground as to why she didn't perform at Ascot. She is tough, and I think running her over a mile you can afford to be aggressive with her. She stays further but her run style is that she doesn't like being pulled around and she shows that in her work at home - if you try sitting in behind horses and asking her to quicken, she doesn't do a lot. She loves being competitive. This time last week Richard Brown [owners' racing manager] called me and said we can't run her on the good to firm ground. Of the course the weather changed and that nice Storm Amy came across to save the day for us! Ascot [Queen Elizabeth II Stakes on QIPCO British Champions Day] is the plan. We spoke beforehand with Richard and said if she performed well today - even if she hadn't won - we would give her a shot at the boys so I can't wait for two weeks' time. I can't imagine Ascot will dry out as quick as a place like Newmarket. It's very tacky and soft there today so I'll say it will be good ground at fastest. The plan is for her to stay in training next year as long as she finishes the season sound. I think Fallen Angel is a mile and a quarter horse really. I am sure she will stay, and we will discuss it next year. We could maybe go for something like the Prix Jean Romanet at Deauville where they might leave us alone in front - that will be interesting - Karl Burke, trainer. Fallen Angel is a strong galloper at a mile. There's a bit of a tailwind today and the ground drying all the time was a slight concern but it's not quick. She just galloped them into the ground -  James Doyle, jockey.", 2025-09-13 - "She's so tough, very honest and James (Doyle) gave Fallen Angel a great ride. She could stay 1m2f but she's probably best ridden aggressively over a mile. The cheekpieces have sharpened her up. She'll go for the Sun Chariot if she's okay and then we could take on the boys at Champions Day at Ascot - Karl Burke, trainer, speaking to Racing TV", 2025-08-03 - "I'm absolutely delighted to have a Group 1 winner for Wathnan, they've been huge supporters and long may it continue. I'm delighted for Danny [Tudhope] as well. I was a little surprised James [Doyle] went the way he did [riding fellow Wathnan runner Crimson Advocate]. I know Crimson Advocate beat us at Ascot but we needed that bit better ground and the cheekpieces made a bit of difference. Also, Fallen Angel's work has taken a step forward since Ascot and especially over the last two weeks. I wouldn't say we were confident but we were bullish going there. She doesn't want any firmness in it like she had at Ascot where, coming round the bend, her head just came up a bit. She's such an honest, game mare and she was battling back in the last 100 yards then, as she did today. She only does what you ask her to do and Aidan's filly coming to her helped her. What I'm really pleased about is that she's emulated Laurens in winning a Group 1 at two, three and four now. The Matron I would imagine is the next port of call and hopefully we'll have some nice ground at that time of year. There is the mile and a quarter Prix Jean Romanet but we'll enjoy today, get her home and see how she is, and then I'll speak to connections, but the way she's won that I'd be happy to stick at a mile - Karl Burke, trainer. Fallen Angel is a superstar. I haven't ridden her since last year when we won the Irish 1,000 Guineas. She means a lot to me and the team as well, that's three Group 1s now as a two-year-old, three-year-old and now as a four-year-old. It's amazing and I have to thank the Wathnan team for giving me the chance to get back on her. She has a good mind on her, a great mentality. Karl [Burke, trainer] always says she never shows a lot at home but she obviously saves it for the track and it's a great day for us all. We went quick. My plan was always to go forwards because she's a straightforward filly. She'd the cheekpieces on today and was a bit lit up in them but she wore them the last day. I thought she was doing a bit too much in my hands but I've always thought she's a filly you couldn't go quick enough on and she's toughed it out. She had to be tough, they were coming at her and it got close at the line, we got headed at one point but she came back. She's a tough filly. She dug deep and got the result and we know the softer the better for her, but she tries so hard every time. She's a superstar - Danny Tudhope, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing.", 2025-06-18 - "Absolutely delighted with Fallen Angel. The ground was probably a little bit quicker than ideal, but I was so pleased with what she did in the last 50 yards - she battled on, and she's just a superstar. I think in the autumn when we get a bit of ease in the ground, she'll be back into a big one for us. We might go up in trip - it will be somewhere between a mile and a mile and a quarter, but ease in the ground is going to be important - Richard Brown, owners' representative.", 2024-05-26 - "We were disappointed with the Guineas. We sent Fallen Angel to Newmarket fit and she didn't blow that much afterwards. The ground maybe was a bit quick on the undulations, but that's looking for excuses, we were beaten fair and square on the day. She just sparked up in the last week, and physically she looked fantastic today - Karl Burke, trainer.", 2023-09-10 - "Fallen Angel jumped great and is a very straightforward ride. You can put her where you want in a race, she stays very well and I thought the last furlong would be her best furlong - Danny Tudhope, rider.", 2023-08-12 - "We were very confident coming here today. Fallen Angel is so laidback and travelled so well in the race. She hit the line strong and will go another furlong no problem. We've always liked her at home and she's quite versatile as regards ground - Daniel Tudhope, jockey."
runner_stable_tour: "She's in great form and looks fantastic. She won't run on ground any quicker than proper good ground. We'll keep an eye on the Dahlia Stakes on Guineas weekend, but she'll definitely go to the Prix d'Ispahan on May 21. She'll have entries at Royal Ascot but we won't run her unless the ground is right. After that there's the Group 1 fillies-only mile race for her. This is likely to be her last season and we're hoping for a swansong at the Breeders' Cup. 31-03-26"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: It's possible last year's winner SEE THE FIRE will have a fitness edge over reappearing pair Fallen Angel and Dancing Rain.
race_betting_forecast: 6/5 See The Fire, 11/4 Fallen Angel, 3/1 Diamond Rain, 7/1 Red Letter
runner_comment: Good form shout and capable fresh but top yard quiet in recent weeks
runner_spotlight: An improved performer last year, winning a good-ground Listed race at Haydock (reappearance) and AW Group 3 at Newcastle around 1m2f before progressing again to place in Grade 1s on firm at Woodbine (1m2f) and Del Mar (1m3f; Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Turf); bang there on form but the fact her top stable has gone nearly three weeks without a winner has to be a slight concern.
runner_quotes: 2025-11-02 - "Diamond Rain delighted us. She's a big filly to get around those turns and she handled herself well. Hopefully next year we'll have some fun with her, hopefully here in America - Charlie Appleby, trainer.", 2025-08-16 - "Diamond Rain has run a great race and was just beaten by a very good filly. I'm not sure where we'll go next, as I feel that Del Mar might be a bit too tight for her, which rules out the Breeders' Cup. We might look at something back in Europe during the autumn, with a bit of juice in the ground - Charlie Appleby, trainer.", 2025-05-24 - "Diamond Rain is a big girl who has strengthened up and has probably improved for another year on her back. She stayed on well and you'd think she'd want further, but Will [Buick] has always said she's got plenty of toe. She hit a bit of a flat spot but once she got rolling I always thought she'd get there - Alex Merriam, trainer's assistant.", 2024-05-18 - "It was a lovely performance. It was a slightly messy race but Diamond Rain did get a lead which was nice and she's stepped up again. This is more of her trip and she doesn't feel like she's anywhere near the finished article. She has a few options and we'll see where she goes next - William Buick, jockey."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: It's possible last year's winner SEE THE FIRE will have a fitness edge over reappearing pair Fallen Angel and Dancing Rain.
race_betting_forecast: 6/5 See The Fire, 11/4 Fallen Angel, 3/1 Diamond Rain, 7/1 Red Letter
runner_owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
runner_comment: Winner of AW Listed at Lingfield before two Group 2 wins at Meydan, but this is harder
runner_spotlight: Gained a breakthrough Listed success at Lingfield (1m5f, AW) on her final 4yo start and added Group 2 wins over 1m1f and 2m at Meydan (both good) in February/March; versatile and talented but will need another career best back in Britain.
runner_quotes: 2026-03-28 - "We ran Fairy Glen in the Balanchine over a mile and one [furlong], which was a bit short for her but she's blossomed since. She had never run two miles before so we were a bit sceptical but she goes on this sort of ground and I though Mickael [Barzalona] rode a sensible race - Ed Crisford, joint-trainer.Fairy Glen was happy throughout the race and when I asked her to pick up she did it nicely - Mickael Barzalona, jockey"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: It's possible last year's winner SEE THE FIRE will have a fitness edge over reappearing pair Fallen Angel and Dancing Rain.
race_betting_forecast: 6/5 See The Fire, 11/4 Fallen Angel, 3/1 Diamond Rain, 7/1 Red Letter
runner_comment: Listed/Group 3 wins last year; solid reappearance; should run well but bit to find on form
runner_spotlight: Winner of a Listed race at Killarney (1m, soft) and Group 3 at the Curragh (1m1f, good to yielding) last summer; creditable third of 12 in 1m2f Group 3 back at the Curragh (soft) on reappearance 25 days ago; likely to give a good account but has a bit to find on ratings.
runner_quotes: 2025-08-30 - "We've just had to be so patient with Red Letter and we have a lovely horse to look forward to next year. Hopefully, she'll fill all of her potential in the next 12 months and is a filly to look forward to. We're looking forward to Champions Weekend, which is very important to us, and we'll have a look at the Blandford Stakes. The ground is very important as she needs ease. Obviously, the family have their say with her but we've been training her with all of this in mind, with both the backend of the season and next year. I think she can get a mile and a half, no problem - Ger Lyons, trainer.", 2024-07-21 - "Red Letter did what we probably expected. She stepped forward nicely from her debut. We will see how she comes out of it. It's just raw ability at the moment. She will tell me where we go with her, whether she comes back for the Moyglare or something like that - Ger Lyons, trainer."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Middleton Fillies' Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: It's possible last year's winner SEE THE FIRE will have a fitness edge over reappearing pair Fallen Angel and Dancing Rain.
race_betting_forecast: 6/5 See The Fire, 11/4 Fallen Angel, 3/1 Diamond Rain, 7/1 Red Letter
runner_comment: Romped home in this last year and the one to beat again on the back of reappearance win
runner_spotlight: Pulverised her seven rivals in this on her second outing of last season, scoring by a whopping 12l on good to firm; couldn't quite reproduce that high-class RPR at Group 1 level in five subsequent outings in 2025 but mostly ran well, including a close second in the Prix de l'Opera at Longchamp (1m2f, soft); didn't have much to spare at the line when 1-3 for a 1m2f Nottingham Listed race on reappearance but cruised through most of that contest and will likely be a lot closer to her best this time; again the one to beat in this.
runner_quotes: 2026-04-26 - "It was always going to be a muddling race and it was my own fault, really. See The Fire moved through the race with ease and we saw plenty of the front, which is never ideal. She's very game, but just needed a bit of company - Rob Hornby, jockey.", 2025-06-18 - "It's another Group 1 place and See The Fire is just a lovely racemare. That should set her up perfectly for the Nassau, which was always her main target - Andrew Balding, trainer.Career best. Race set up for a closer, it was an end-to-end gallop. Good run - Oisin Murphy, rider of See The Fire.", 2025-05-15 - "We've always had a very high opinion of See The Fire and she's improved since last year. We were a little disappointed with her run at Sandown but she probably needed it as she travelled well but didn't quite get home, that probably put her spot on for today. She's a very exciting horse. We always fancied going back for the Nassau as she was second in it last year and that's the primary objective. If that goes well we might consider coming back here in August. We made a bit of a b******s of campaigning her last year when not stepping up in trip until it was too late. She obviously relishes this trip. She really found her feet after her Eclipse run last year and ran really well on Champions Day, having had a busy season. There's lots of look forward to - Andrew Balding, trainer.", 2024-08-24 - "See The Fire was very good at Goodwood and deserved to win a decent race. Hopefully she'll now go on to better things. We don't need to remind people who her dam was when winning the Juddmonte, so we know she was bred for the job. She actually likes not having space, although she's not overly-big. She enjoys tracking on to the back of them like she did at Goodwood, but then when it opened up she was electric. I'd definitely say we'll be going for a Group 1 next - Oisin Murphy, rider.", 2024-08-01 - "It was a good run from See The Fire. I thought it was a pace collapse from two furlongs out and I felt I'd pick up Ryan, but as he taught me in the last race, he takes a bit of passing - Oisin Murphy, rider.", 2023-10-13 - "See The Fire travelled strongly, but she got there sooner than Oisin wanted to. She just tired late on but she is an exciting filly, and we are looking forward to next year. I think we will look towards the 1,000 Guineas with her - Andrew Balding, trainer.", 2023-08-12 - "See The Fire is the best specimen we've had out of Arabian Queen so far and we came here confident as her homework had been exceptional. We'll probably stick to seven furlongs for now, but she has a big future all right - David Bowe, owner's racing manager."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: The O'Brien first string is CHRISTMAS DAY, whose Ballysax win earns him narrow preference over Morshdi and Item.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Christmas Day, 7/2 Morshdi, 6/1 Action,  Item, 8/1 King's Trail, 11/1 Wise Prince, 14/1 Al Zanati, 20/1 Guildmaster
runner_owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
runner_comment: Sluggish 4th with tongue-tie in Classic Trial at Sandown; the form pick if he bounces back
runner_spotlight: Closely related to 2025 Derby winner Lambourn; carries head high but progressed with every start last season and ended it with a close, battling second to one of his stablemates in the Group 1 Futurity at Doncaster (1m, heavy); consequently 4-6 for Group 3 Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good) three weeks ago but sluggish leaving the stalls and for most of the race before mustering something to finish fourth of seven; perhaps a first-time tongue-tie that day backfired, and he now goes without; the form pick if he bounces back.
runner_quotes: 2025-08-02 - "Action is quite a big horse, he stays well and ideally I would have loved to have gone a stride quicker but he was having such a look in front I was letting him find his feet and get comfortable. I thought the last 50 yards he hit the line well. He is going to learn plenty from today - Wayne Lordan, jockey, speaking to Racing TV."
runner_stable_tour: "He ran a very good race in the Futurity Trophy when second to Hawk Mountain and he'll be trained for a Derby. I'd imagine it'll be Epsom as he looks an Epsom type. He'll run in a trial, probably at Chester or Leopardstown. 31-03-26"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: The O'Brien first string is CHRISTMAS DAY, whose Ballysax win earns him narrow preference over Morshdi and Item.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Christmas Day, 7/2 Morshdi, 6/1 Action,  Item, 8/1 King's Trail, 11/1 Wise Prince, 14/1 Al Zanati, 20/1 Guildmaster
runner_comment: 11-2 for Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good) and keeping on steadily from rear to be 2nd
runner_spotlight: Won debut last August and a 1m1f AW Group 3 at Chantilly in November; runner-up in British Group 3s in his other two starts, on reappearance starting 11-2 for Group 3 Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good; unraced on softer) and keeping on steadily from rear to be second of seven, with Wise Prince and Action the next two behind him; that's not the best form in this field but he could still bring potential.
runner_quotes: 2025-10-11 - "Al Zanati is hard to teach as he would never do that at home, but he has another run under his belt and that should man him up - Charlie Appleby, trainer."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: The O'Brien first string is CHRISTMAS DAY, whose Ballysax win earns him narrow preference over Morshdi and Item.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Christmas Day, 7/2 Morshdi, 6/1 Action,  Item, 8/1 King's Trail, 11/1 Wise Prince, 14/1 Al Zanati, 20/1 Guildmaster
runner_prev_owners: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith (own_575284) - 2025-09-16, Magnier,Tabor,Smith,Westerberg,Brant (own_1266184) - 2025-09-09, Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith (own_575284) - 2025-08-21, Magnier,Tabor,Smith,Westerberg,Brant (own_1266184) - 2025-08-12
runner_comment: Won 1m2f Group 3 Ballysax last month; will stay further but looks firmly in the mix
runner_spotlight: Won a heavy-ground maiden on third 2yo start and led on the post in Group 3 at Leopardstown (1m1f, yielding) in October; 11-1 with three of his stablemates among those at shorter odds when he reappeared in nine-runner Group 3 Ballysax at Leopardstown (1m2f, good to yielding) last month but it was him who asserted in the final 1f to win by half a length; will stay further but looks firmly in the mix.
runner_quotes: 2026-04-12 - "Wayne [Lordan] was very happy with Christmas Day, he gave him a lovely ride. He won a Group race around here with Jack [Cleary] and then we were going to a Group One with him next time in France and he got a temperature and didn't get to go - Aidan O'Brien, trainer", 2025-09-20 - "Christmas Day obviously had a lovely run the first day, but things just didn't go right for him in a good maiden at Tipperary. Once he fills his frame he'll be a big, strong, imposing three-year-old - Chris Armstrong, trainer's representative."
runner_stable_tour: "He made good progress last season and showed a good attitude to win the Eyrefield on his final run. He'll start off in a Derby trial. 31-03-26"

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: The O'Brien first string is CHRISTMAS DAY, whose Ballysax win earns him narrow preference over Morshdi and Item.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Christmas Day, 7/2 Morshdi, 6/1 Action,  Item, 8/1 King's Trail, 11/1 Wise Prince, 14/1 Al Zanati, 20/1 Guildmaster
runner_comment: Won debut; 3rd in Newmarket novice last month; requires a big step forward but early days
runner_spotlight: Favourite for novice at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and eventually got on top despite looking green; perhaps didn't make as much improvement as expected when third of five almost throughout in novice at Newmarket (1m2f, good; 100-30) four weeks ago, which leaves him requiring a big step forward in this contest but that track may not have suited; early days still.
runner_quotes: 2025-12-22 - "He's a nice horse. He's a work in progress. The lads said that Guildmaster spent the whole time on his back legs in the pre-parade ring. He was green in the race as well, on and off the bridle, and will definitely get ten furlongs. He might be the type to run in another novice with a penalty - Robert Havlin, jockey, speaking to Sky Sports Racing."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: The O'Brien first string is CHRISTMAS DAY, whose Ballysax win earns him narrow preference over Morshdi and Item.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Christmas Day, 7/2 Morshdi, 6/1 Action,  Item, 8/1 King's Trail, 11/1 Wise Prince, 14/1 Al Zanati, 20/1 Guildmaster
runner_comment: 2-2 as 2yo; a lot to find on ratings but a good prospect; looks sure to be suited by 1m2f+
runner_spotlight: Well on top last September in a maiden at Kempton (7f, AW) and four-runner novice at Bath (1m, good to firm); non-runner in Group 1 Futurity in October due to heavy going; this Frankel colt has a lot to find on ratings but he's a fascinating prospect who looks sure to be suited by 1m2f+.
runner_quotes: 2025-09-30 - "It's a difficult track, but the key today was to bring Item here and educate him regardless of where he finished. He showed a lot of ability and has a big engine. He's learnt as much before and after as he did in the race, because he probably finds galloping the easiest part. It's hard to know exactly what level he's run to, but the feel he gave me and the way he took it all in is exciting for connections. Doncaster [for the Futurity] will really suit him and he certainly won't be the worst horse in the race. He found the line well today without me getting too serious on him. With his big, beautiful stride I don't think softer ground will be an issue - Rob Hornby, talking to Sky Sports Racing."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: The O'Brien first string is CHRISTMAS DAY, whose Ballysax win earns him narrow preference over Morshdi and Item.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Christmas Day, 7/2 Morshdi, 6/1 Action,  Item, 8/1 King's Trail, 11/1 Wise Prince, 14/1 Al Zanati, 20/1 Guildmaster
runner_comment: Likely to stay 1m2f but needs to prove that his run in 2,000 Guineas (13-2) was all wrong
runner_spotlight: Left good impression with 1m wins on Kempton AW in December and March, the latter in a good conditions stakes; just 13-2 for the 2,000 Guineas at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago and he was bang there 3f out to 2f out, but floundered thereafter and finished ninth of 14; likely to stay 1m2f but needs to prove that his run in that recent Classic was all wrong.
runner_quotes: 2026-03-28 - "I've been lucky enough to sit on King's Trail a couple of times at home and he was impressive when he won here the first time. It was a very messy race and he'll have learned plenty from the first couple of furlongs. He's straightforward and has a great mind for a Sea The Stars. I had to wake him up a little to get a position, but you couldn't fault his attitude at all. He's done nothing wrong and I'm sure he'll have a few stiffer targets now - Billy Loughnane, speaking on Racing TV."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)
race_verdict: The O'Brien first string is CHRISTMAS DAY, whose Ballysax win earns him narrow preference over Morshdi and Item.
race_betting_forecast: 2/1 Christmas Day, 7/2 Morshdi, 6/1 Action,  Item, 8/1 King's Trail, 11/1 Wise Prince, 14/1 Al Zanati, 20/1 Guildmaster
runner_comment: Won here on second start as 2yo and 1m1f Listed race at Newmarket four weeks ago; player
runner_spotlight: Brother to 1m2f and 14.5f (Group 2) winner Nakheel (RPR 110); won here (1m, good) on second start as 2yo and followed up in clearcut style in seven-runner Listed race at Newmarket (1m1f, good) four weeks ago; non-runner in 1m2f Listed race at Chester last week given the problem they had with the track; he has one of the best chances on form in this field and is on his way up.
runner_quotes: 2026-04-15 - "Morshdi has the Dee Stakes written all over him, even if he now has a penalty. We have a sister to the second at home, so that's another plus -  Tom Marquand, jockey."

race_race_name: Al Basti Equiworld Dubai Dante Stakes (Group 2)

runner_comment: Kept on for third in Classic Trial at Sandown; could still be a good prospect
runner_spotlight: Scored comfortably in six-runner novice at Nottingham (1m, good to soft) in October on his only start as 2yo; 6-1 for Group 3 Classic Trial at Sandown (1m2f, good) on return three weeks ago and kept on to split Al Zanati and Action in third of seven, having raced freely early on; major work to do and something to learn, but he could still be a good prospect.

race_race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Westow Stakes (Listed Race)
race_verdict: Tempting to side with C&D and Group 2 winner REVIVAL POWER (nap), who is treading a similar path to her Nunthorpe-winning sister.
race_betting_forecast: 11/4 Aspect Island, 3/1 Manatee Mehmas,  Revival Power, 7/1 Simplify, 9/1 Dickensian, 12/1 Black Star Boy, 20/1 Exclamation, 25/1 Wor Faayth, 33/1 Boston Dan, 50/1 Palmeira,  Temple Of Athena
runner_comment: Third in G3 and Grade 1 (again 5f) last term; solid return last month; sets the standard
runner_spotlight: Won Yarmouth nursery (6f, soft) in September but better form when third in Group 3 Cornwallis at Newmarket (good) and Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint (again 5f, firm) the following month; looked to have trained on when beaten around 2l in all-aged Group 3 at Newmarket four weeks ago; holds Group 1 entries over 5f and 6f at Royal Ascot and now drops back into his own age group; sets the standard.
runner_quotes: 2025-10-31 - "It was a fantastic run. Aspect Island is improving all the time and he's come a long way since Yarmouth. Tasleet improved; he got quicker. He's a big horse, but he handled this well. We've learned a bit and he's learned a good bit as well, so we'll be looking forward to next year with him - James Owen, trainer."

race_race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Westow Stakes (Listed Race)
race_verdict: Tempting to side with C&D and Group 2 winner REVIVAL POWER (nap), who is treading a similar path to her Nunthorpe-winning sister.

runner_comment: Good step forward on first go at 5f with h'cap win on return; unexposed and can do better
runner_spotlight: Newbury maiden (good to soft) winner who was gelded after finishing down the field in Listed event here (also 6f) in October; coped well with the drop to 5f when taking good step forward to land Sandown handicap (good) on his reappearance three weeks ago; unexposed and likely capable of better again.
runner_quotes: 2026-04-24 - "Black Star Boy is a nice horse. We ran him in a Listed race last year and we were disappointed but he didn't behave great, got upset in the stalls and pulled hard. He's been gelded and the guys at home have done lots of work with him at the stalls and he was really good today. He's growing up and hopefully he's an exciting sprinter - Ed Walker, trainer, speaking to Racing TV.", 2025-09-19 - "Black Star Boy is in the Goffs Million next week, which is worth a fortune, and we bought him with that race in mind. It's probably not the right thing to do because he's such a big horse, but it's half a million euros to the winner so we'll see - Ed Walker, trainer."

race_race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Westow Stakes (Listed Race)
race_verdict: Tempting to side with C&D and Group 2 winner REVIVAL POWER (nap), who is treading a similar path to her Nunthorpe-winning sister.

She's one of those fantastic horses to train, you're lucky to get one like this. She's very good - Tim Easterby, trainer.", 2025-05-17 - "She'll improve massively. Revival Power has been really backward in her coat and looks backward still. We haven't been hard on her. She's a lovely filly. She'll go to Ascot, for the Queen Mary I think - Tim Easterby, trainer."

race_verdict: The suggestion is LIGHTKEEPER who is rated 128 over fences but was second in this grade at Bellewstown last year when last over hurdles
race_betting_forecast: 3/1 Lightkeeper,  Rocky's Howya, 4/1 Jerandme, 5/1 You Done Well, 12/1 God Of Thunder,  Woodstream Lad, 14/1 Solly Attwell, 18/1 Circus Act, 25/1 Spinning Web, 33/1 Midnight Eagle, 50/1 Anysignofhim
runner_comment: Runner-up in a C&D claimer when last seen over hurdles; can go well fresh; one to consider
runner_spotlight: Not heavily raced for his age with eight wins between all disciplines; was runner-up in a C&D claimer when last seen over hurdles and had one solid AW Flat run since; can go well fresh and is one to consider.
runner_quotes: 2020-09-14 - "Sean Flanagan said Jerandme had settled down an awful lot and basically Sean has been the making of him. He did exactly what Sean said. He settled, he jumped well and he actually won with a bit of speed today - Denis O'Regan, rider", 2020-09-04 - "Jerandme is a very geniuine horse and he never lets us down, no matter what we run him in. He stays very well and maybe next year he could be a contender for the Ascot Stakes. The extra trip today was a help. The Irish Cesarewitch could be the next stop - Noel Meade, trainer.", 2019-05-13 - "He's a nice summer horse and bounces off that ground. He's a bit bubbly and the small field helped him here. It's a lovely track to be able to ride him like that, getting him relaxed. Sean [Flanagan] gave him a beautiful ride and got him into contention without having to buzz him up. At Cork last year he just threw his head in the air and flicked hurdles out of it, didn't lift a leg. I was worried that might happen again when he came to the track but hopefully he comes home sound and can run in novice hurdles through the summer. The reason we gave him time off was because he spent the whole of last season in training and ran into the summer, we felt he wouldn't go on heavy and then waited until now - Noel Meade, trainer of Jerandme", 2018-06-12 - "I'd say he's a nice horse. He pulled hard and was green but he's not slow - Mark O'Hare, rider"

runner_prev_owners: Premier Racing Club (own_923708) - 2024-01-22, Corner House Syndicate (own_244648) - 2024-01-11
runner_comment: Made almost all to win this race last year, has been running on the Flat, could go well
runner_spotlight: Made almost all to win this race last year (Ballycashin third); ran well off 10lb higher mark at Tramore in August; otherwise has done all his racing on the Flat over the past year; placed on multiple occasions, fair form late; fourth of nine at Ballinrobe last Friday.
runner_quotes: 2024-08-18 - "Expound has plenty of ability and it's good the way he can mix it between both [codes]. When he won his [Flat] maiden he made the running and Denis [Hogan, trainer] said to keep it simple, he likes to make the running and let the horses try and catch him. He was very good from the front, jumped really well and travelled and when they came to him he picked up and done it nicely in the end - Daniel King, jockey, speaking to RTV."

runner_spotlight: Has recorded her only two wins over hurdles at this venue, both over longer distances than this; stuck to her task over 2m4f here last October; failed to exploit a much lower chase mark here the following month and absent since.
runner_quotes: 2025-10-23 - "Ballycashin was disappointing in Punchestown the last day. I have been hitting the crossbar on her a few times, so a big thanks to Tony and the owners for keeping me on her. She seems to handle the track and it brings out the best in her. I got there plenty soon, but I knew she stayed well at that trip, so I wanted to use that and I had race fitness on my side - Anna McGuinness, jockey.", 2022-10-30 - "Ballycashin will go hurdling now even though she isn't the biggest. She is tough - Tony Mullins, trainer."

runner_prev_owners: Mrs Helen Mullins (own_360124) - 2025-07-31, Mrs Rita Byrne (own_467756) - 2025-04-03
runner_comment: A long time since his last win, went well for this rider when fourth at Wexford
runner_spotlight: No win since 2021; has occasionally run well in defeat; improved from a run on the Flat at Navan in March when going well for this jockey (on her first ride over jumps) at Wexford last month; beaten 8l in fourth, so needs to raise his game another notch.
runner_quotes: 2021-03-25 - "Takarengo is a very good jumper but he can lose lengths at times and I'm not sure if hurdling is for him. If the owner wants to run him at Punchestown or somewhere like that then maybe we can look at that but I think the main target is the Cesarewitch, which he was third in last year. There's a few nice pots between now and then and he needs a bit of nice ground, he's not a good-to-firm ground horse. He was in real good order and today was his day to win a race over hurdles - Tom Mullins, trainer.", 2019-10-19 - "Today was all about getting him right for either next weekend or the weekend after. There's a 50 grand handicap here next weekend and there's a 100 grand one in Naas the following week. He'll get a nice little slap from the handicapper now, I suppose he's entitled to, and it'll give him a decent weight in either handicap. Then we'll think about hurdling or something. he could do anything. He could even be an Ebor horse for next year and he could go further - Tom Mullins, trainer", 2019-09-02 - "We were fancying him coming here. A mile and a half or two miles would be no problem to him. I thought the ground would be worse than it was, they say it's heavy but I don't think it is. When the two horses ran off the bend it wasn't a help, making his own running is the last thing this lad wants. We'll try to sneak into a good handicap over a mile and six or two miles. He's too low for the Cesarewitch but the front two today came away from the rest - Ben [Coen] said they came to him twice and he pulled out more for him twice. More than likely he'll go hurdling in time, he's a big raw horse - Tom Mullins, trainer of Takarengo"
